frameworks.name: neural population geometry
frameworks.one_line: geometry and dimensionality of task representations in neocortex

frameworks.name: continual / task learning
frameworks.one_line: how new tasks are acquired with minimal mutual interference

frameworks.name: supervised + unsupervised learning hybrids
frameworks.one_line: ML schemes combining the two to model brain task partitioning

core_question: How do brains and artificial agents acquire and code multiple tasks while minimising mutual interference?
key_assumptions: neural representational geometry reflects how knowledge is partitioned across tasks
ideas from machine learning can illuminate biological continual learning
key_findings: review synthesises brain recording studies of representational change during task learning
highlights changes in geometry and dimensionality of neocortical task codes
interpretations: task knowledge is partitioned via specific representational structures that limit interference
combined supervised/unsupervised ML models help explain natural task learning
